title: The Work of Creation, Part 4
speaker: Pastor Tito Lyro
source_files: The Work of Creation_ Part 4 (Pastor Tito Lyro).txt
outline: Introduction: Addressing the topic of evolution in the creation series
The biblical and confessional teaching: six-day creation
Common evolutionary claims and their challenges
Scientific and theological responses to evolution
Why the question of origins matters for theology
The integrity of Scripture on creation
Conclusion: Trusting God's Word over human theories
summary: Pastor Tito Lyro addresses the topic of evolution in light of the biblical doctrine of creation, examining the claim that God created the universe in six days. He interacts with various views and defends the plain reading of Scripture.
transcript:
## What Is Evolution?

We study evolution in the context of creation. How we answer the questions *"Where did we come from?"* and *"Why are we here?"* provides the basis for our entire worldview — our view of human dignity, morality, law, and meaning. And we should also be concerned that truth is not being taught. Even if your own children do not attend public schools, the future members of this church and the future leaders of this country are largely being formed in an educational system built on atheistic assumptions.

The theory of evolution is commonly used synonymously with **naturalism** — the idea that nature is all there is. If you have ever questioned why anti-depressants have become so pervasive, consider this: the philosophical undergirding of modern psychiatry is that biology is all there is. That is a direct consequence of evolutionary thinking.

The theory of evolution as described by Darwin refers to **biological evolution**: the formation of new species through gradual change. What has largely replaced Darwinism in serious science is **neo-Darwinism**, which incorporates genetics. The basic idea: randomly occurring mutations introduce small changes to an organism, and natural selection retains those changes that give the organism some reproductive advantage. According to evolutionary theory, the ultimate goal of every species is reproduction — changes that make a species more fertile are the ones preserved.

This creates an obvious tension with certain modern ideologies that claim to be grounded in biology. If evolutionary fitness is measured by reproductive capacity, then homosexuality and transgenderism — by the theory's own logic — represent *devolution*, not evolution. The worldview cannot consistently support the claims made in its name.

## Two Models, Same Evidence

There are really only two models to explain the origins of all things: evolution and creation. Both sides have access to the same evidence. The difference is in the assumptions brought to that evidence.

- The **evolutionary assumption**: there is no God; therefore, everything must be explained naturalistically.
- The **creationist assumption**: there is a God; therefore, everything should be interpreted in light of that fact.

Neither side is unbiased. The scientific method itself carries an assumption: that only things observable and repeatable in a laboratory count as real knowledge. A transcendent God cannot be observed and repeated in a lab — so that assumption is itself a form of atheism.

**What is true about evolution (micro-evolution):** Natural selection does lead to formation of new species — populations that do not normally interbreed. Farmers have known for centuries that cross-breeding can produce stronger strains. Modern creation scientists believe the created *kind* was at a level above species — that the "dog kind" (Latin: *canis*) was created, and wolves, German shepherds, and poodles all developed from that original kind. This is micro-evolution, or adaptation, and it is real and observable.

**What is unproven about evolution (macro-evolution):** Change beyond the kind level — a reptile becoming a bird, a fish becoming a land animal — has never been demonstrated. Serious scientists acknowledge that macro-evolution is a hypothesis requiring further research. Even the best evolutionary scientists offer three main evidences: micro-evolution (which creationists accept), the fossil record, and imperfections in nature. As we will see, none of these establish macro-evolution.

## The Appeal and the Roots of Evolutionary Philosophy

The appeal of evolutionary philosophy is ultimately this: **no God, therefore no accountability.** Evolution provides an excuse for people to justify self-centered behavior. It is part of a long war against God that has been fought throughout history.

Several major ideological systems are grounded in evolutionary theory:

- **Humanism** and **secularism** — the idea that humanity is the measure of all things.
- **Communism** — Karl Marx himself described his system as Darwinism applied to society. The "evolved man" is the communist man. There has never been a Christian communist country, because at its core, communism denies the God who creates and redeems.

Our laws increasingly reflect this evolutionary thinking. How do we decide if something should be a law? By a vote. We are saying: whatever most people think is right, is right. That is moral relativism — the idea that right and wrong are determined by shifting societal consensus rather than by the transcendent standard of God's word.

## Reasons Against Evolution

**The Second Law of Thermodynamics.** The law of entropy teaches that when left to themselves, systems always move from order toward disorder, from complex toward simple. Evolution requires the opposite: a movement from simple to enormously complex. The universe is running down — stars are dying, energy is being consumed. Evolutionary scientists try to get around this by claiming the sun provides sufficient energy to drive increasing complexity on earth, but undirected energy does not create order any more than a blowtorch applied to a flat-pack box of furniture will assemble it. Directed energy — a designer — is required.

**Information theory.** The information content required to describe the structure and operation of a human being is vastly more complex than even the simplest bacterium. The field of information theory has demonstrated mathematically that the coded programs in biological cells could never arise by random chance. Statisticians have shown that it is more likely for a monkey randomly typing on a typewriter to produce all the plays of Shakespeare than for the complexity of a human cell to develop randomly. This is why the amount of time required for evolutionary processes keeps expanding — more time is the only variable evolutionary theory can offer in response to the math. But statistically, adding more time does not change the fundamental impossibility.

**The gap between life and non-life.** DNA and RNA must both be present simultaneously for life to continue. The origin of life from non-life has never been demonstrated. When scientists come closest, they use complex, precisely directed laboratory procedures — which itself demonstrates that intelligence and intention are required for life to arise.

**Fruit fly experiments.** Evolutionists use fruit flies for experiments because a fruit fly's entire life cycle is about twenty-four hours. Studies have followed over 10,000 generations of fruit flies — the equivalent of hundreds of millions of years in human terms — while introducing radiation and other mutation-causing agents. After 10,000 generations, with every kind of induced mutation, the result was: fruit flies. Some with extra eyes, some missing legs, some deformed — but always fruit flies. Complexity from simplicity cannot be demonstrated.

## The Spectrum of Belief Within Creationism

It is worth noting that not all who call themselves creationists agree on every point. Organizations like the Institute for Creation Research, the Creation Research Society, and Answers in Genesis hold to six literal twenty-four-hour days of creation, a young earth (roughly 10,000 to 100,000 years old), and a global flood. This is where our church stands, as the most faithful reading of Scripture and our confessional standards.

Others, such as the organization Reasons to Believe, believe the days of creation represent very long periods while still affirming that God intervened repeatedly — a form of progressive creationism or guided theistic evolution. And outside explicitly Christian circles, the intelligent design movement argues from the evidence of design without necessarily affirming biblical creation.

The ultimate appeal of atheistic evolution is: no God, no accountability, no standard above human preference. The ultimate appeal of biblical creation is: God made us, God redeems us, God is the measure of all things — and we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works. That is a far better account of why we are here.
